channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Microsoft beefs up Remote Desktop security with ... hard-to-read messages
item.description: Microsoft has updated its Remote Desktop to enhance security against phishing attacks, but users are experiencing issues with unreadable warning messages. This update is significant as it aims to protect users from potential security threats.

item.title: It's a myth that you need Mythos to find bugs: Open source models can do it just as well
item.description: Ari Herbert-Voss, OpenAI's first security hire, argues that open source models can effectively identify bugs, potentially enhancing security without job losses. This perspective challenges the notion that proprietary models are necessary for effective bug detection.

item.title: I've earned nearly $700 just by using Rakuten - here's how I did it
item.description: An article discusses how the author earned nearly $700 using Rakuten, highlighting tips for maximizing cashback when shopping online. This information could help consumers save money while shopping.

item.title: Trump to UK: Stop taxing our big beautiful tech corps or face tariff tsunami
item.description: Donald Trump has threatened the UK with tariffs if it does not repeal its Digital Services Tax on large US tech companies, reigniting a long-standing dispute over taxation of tech giants. This could impact US-UK trade relations significantly.

item.title: The best inventory management software of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A review of the best inventory management software for 2026 highlights options like Odoo, Square, and Katana, providing insights for businesses seeking improved stock management. This is crucial for optimizing supply chain operations.

item.title: Does Amazon price match other retailers? What to know
item.description: The article explores whether Amazon price matches with other retailers like Walmart and Best Buy, clarifying the company's policies on price matching. Understanding these policies can help consumers make informed purchasing decisions.

item.title: The Men Behind Your Favorite AI Gay Thirst Traps
item.description: A viral moment featuring Instagram influencers has brought attention to a group of attractive individuals who are not real, highlighting the phenomenon of AI-generated personas. This raises questions about authenticity in social media.

item.title: Greece relaxes Euro biometric border entry rules amid airport chaos
item.description: Greece is easing its biometric border entry rules after the implementation of the EU's Entry/Exit System caused missed flights for some travelers. This adjustment aims to alleviate chaos at airports.

item.title: The best website builder for SEO in 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: The article reviews the best website builders for SEO in 2026, featuring platforms like Wix, Webflow, and Shopify. This information is valuable for businesses looking to enhance their online visibility.

item.title: 5 Reasons to Think Twice Before Using ChatGPT—or Any Chatbot—for Financial Advice
item.description: As reliance on AI chatbots for financial advice grows, the article warns users to approach such guidance with skepticism. This caution is important as it highlights the limitations of AI in sensitive areas like finance.

item.title: Health-care AI is here. We don’t know if it actually helps patients.
item.description: AI is increasingly being used in healthcare for tasks like notetaking and interpreting medical results, but its actual benefits for patients remain uncertain. This raises questions about the effectiveness and reliability of AI in critical medical settings.

item.title: UK gov pays public £550 to discuss Digital ID – then bans journalists from the room
item.description: The UK government is paying citizens to discuss a national digital ID system, but has restricted journalists from attending these discussions. This raises concerns about transparency and the authenticity of public input.

item.title: Betting shop bug ends in kidnap plot as staff turn ransom artists
item.description: A computer glitch in a Spanish betting shop led to duplicate jackpots and resulted in the kidnapping of the store manager for ransom. This bizarre incident underscores the potential chaos caused by tech failures.

item.title: Government adoption of AI agents could outpace the private sector
item.description: A survey indicates that government leaders believe AI will have a greater impact on the workforce than previous technologies like the internet and smartphones. This suggests a shift in how AI is perceived in terms of its potential influence.

item.title: To fix this Wi-Fi network, we'll need a crane
item.description: A tech support story highlights the challenges of fixing a Wi-Fi network that requires heavy lifting, showcasing the sometimes absurd lengths needed to resolve tech issues. It reflects the humorous side of tech support.

item.title: Researchers find cyber-sabotage malware that may predate Stuxnet by five years
item.description: Researchers have discovered a malware called FAST16 that may predate the infamous Stuxnet, suggesting it was an early form of cyber sabotage aimed at engineering software. This discovery raises concerns about the history of cyber threats.

item.title: China’s DeepSeek previews new AI model a year after jolting US rivals
item.description: Chinese AI firm DeepSeek has previewed its new AI model, V4, claiming it can compete with top US models. This development could shift the competitive landscape in AI technology.

item.title: Prestigious photo contest answers ‘what is a photo?’
item.description: The World Press Photo competition is exploring the definition of photography in the age of generative AI. This reflects ongoing debates about authenticity and the nature of visual storytelling.

item.title: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use
item.description: DeepSeek's V4 model features a million-token context, enhancing its usability for AI agents. This advancement could improve how AI interacts with complex data.

item.title: OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 in Microsoft Foundry: Frontier intelligence on an enterprise ready platform
item.description: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 is now available in Microsoft Foundry, offering advanced AI capabilities for enterprise applications. This integration signifies a step forward in making powerful AI tools accessible for business use.

item.title: trag-cli 2.1.0
item.description: The release of trag-cli 2.1.0 introduces an automatic context builder for Claude, enhancing coding projects by simplifying context management. This tool aims to streamline development processes for programmers.

item.title: agsuperbrain added to PyPI
item.description: The Super-Brain tool has been added to PyPI, providing a local graph and vector intelligence to enhance AI coding assistants' memory capabilities. This addition aims to improve codebase management and productivity.

item.title: AI drives software productivity – and challenges – for Motorway
item.description: Ryan Cormack from Motorway discusses how AI is transforming software productivity in their used car marketplace, highlighting both the speed benefits and the challenges faced by engineering teams. This reflects the dual nature of AI integration in business.

item.title: Anthropic Using ‘Fear-Based Marketing’ to Promote Claude Mythos: Sam Altman
item.description: Sam Altman criticized Anthropic's marketing tactics, suggesting that fears surrounding its AI model, Claude, may be overstated, particularly regarding its cybersecurity features. This commentary sheds light on competitive tensions in the AI industry.

item.title: claude-usage-widget 0.6.1
item.description: The claude-usage-widget 0.6.1 provides a desktop widget and CLI for tracking real-time usage limits and costs associated with Claude Code. This tool aims to help users manage their AI resource consumption effectively.

item.title: Fantastic combo! Street Fighter cast tease movie's Easter eggs, as they say there's "like, a hundred" for game fans to spot
item.description: The upcoming Street Fighter movie features numerous Easter eggs for fans to discover, as teased by the cast. This adds an interactive element for viewers, enhancing the movie experience.

item.title: tokenxray 0.3.17
item.description: Tokenxray 0.3.17 allows users to analyze their AI coding session costs and track token usage, helping to identify unexpected expenses. This tool is aimed at improving financial transparency in AI projects.

item.title: IWT_PyTorch 0.1.2
item.description: IWT_PyTorch 0.1.2 introduces a feature selection classifier based on PyTorch and scikit-learn, aimed at enhancing machine learning model performance. This tool is significant for data scientists looking to optimize their models.

item.title: claude-usage-widget 0.6.0
item.description: The claude-usage-widget 0.6.0, similar to version 0.6.1, provides a desktop widget and CLI for monitoring Claude Code usage limits and costs, aiding users in managing their AI expenses.

item.title: ChatGPT predicted the first round of the NFL Draft and here's what it said
item.description: In a mock draft contest, ChatGPT's predictions for the NFL Draft's first round were compared against a human drafter, showcasing AI's capabilities in sports forecasting. This highlights AI's growing role in sports analytics.

item.title: Microsoft explored buying Cursor before SpaceX lined up a $60 billion deal
item.description: Microsoft considered acquiring Cursor before SpaceX finalized a $60 billion deal for the AI startup, which includes options for partnership. This highlights the competitive landscape for AI startups and the strategic interests of major tech companies.

item.title: OnePlus quietly drops a great smartwatch while its global business is in limbo
item.description: OnePlus has quietly discontinued its titanium Watch 4 smartwatch amid uncertainty about its global business operations. This move raises questions about the company's future in the tech market.

item.title: Realbotix to Showcase Humanoid Robot at Bitcoin 2026 Conference
item.description: Realbotix will showcase a humanoid robot at the Bitcoin 2026 Conference, demonstrating its AI capabilities for customer interaction. This event highlights the growing intersection of AI and robotics in various industries.

item.title: rhwp-python added to PyPI
item.description: The rhwp-python package has been added to PyPI, providing Python bindings for parsing and rendering HWP/HWPX documents. This addition enhances the tools available for developers working with Korean word processor formats.

item.title: Chaos reinstates support for AMD GPUs in V-Ray
item.description: Chaos has reinstated support for AMD GPUs in its V-Ray render engine using AMD's HIP framework, allowing for better performance comparisons with NVIDIA GPUs. This update is significant for users relying on AMD hardware.

item.title: nvd-claude-proxy 0.8.8
item.description: The nvd-claude-proxy 0.8.8 allows users to run Claude Code and other Anthropic SDK clients on NVIDIA NIM models through a local proxy. This update improves accessibility for developers using NVIDIA's technology.

item.title: Blackstone Sees ‘Best Year Ever’ for Its IPOs as Earnings Beat
item.description: Blackstone reported record earnings driven by a strong IPO market and AI ventures, despite geopolitical tensions. The company anticipates continued growth in AI-related investments.

item.title: esp-easy-ai added to PyPI
item.description: The esp-easy-ai package has been added to PyPI, offering a JSON-configured model training and export pipeline for ESP32 devices. This tool enhances the development of AI applications on microcontrollers.

item.title: paddlecodec 0.2.0
item.description: Paddlecodec 0.2.0 has been released as a video decoder for PyTorch, providing new capabilities for developers working with video data in machine learning projects. This enhances PyTorch's functionality in multimedia processing.

item.title: I thought NotebookLM was getting worse, but these 4 features proved me wrong
item.description: NotebookLM has introduced four new features that improve its functionality, countering previous concerns about its performance. This suggests ongoing development and refinement of the AI tool.

item.title: router-maestro 0.1.34
item.description: Router-maestro 0.1.34 is a new multi-model routing and load balancing system that features an OpenAI-compatible API, enhancing its usability for developers.

item.title: The Ultimate Travel Destination Each Zodiac Should Add To Their Vision Board
item.description: This article suggests travel destinations based on zodiac signs, highlighting adventurous options like New Zealand for Aries.

item.title: Parents Can Now Easily Monitor Teen Interactions With Meta AI in Canada
item.description: Meta has launched a new feature in Canada allowing parents to monitor their teens' interactions with Meta AI, enhancing parental oversight.

item.title: The Facts About Google Click Signals, Rankings, And SEO via @sejournal, @martinibuster
item.description: The article explains how Google uses click data to influence SEO rankings, providing insights into its algorithms and their impact on search results.

item.title: rst2gemtext 0.6.0
item.description: rst2gemtext 0.6.0 is a tool that converts reStructuredText to Gemtext, facilitating content formatting for Gemini markup.

item.title: Microsoft partnership has "limited our ability": Leaked memo reveals OpenAI's open hostility towards one of its biggest investors
item.description: A leaked memo reveals OpenAI's frustration with its partnership with Microsoft, indicating that a new collaboration with Amazon may improve its market position.

item.title: SchoolAI Earns ESSA Tier 3 Certification, Backed by Research Showing 28% Critical Thinking Gain
item.description: SchoolAI has received ESSA Tier 3 Certification, supported by research showing a 28% gain in critical thinking among students using the platform.

item.title: Helping Parents Understand the Conversations Their Teens Are Having With AI
item.description: Meta is introducing tools to help parents understand their teens' conversations with AI, enhancing transparency and communication.

item.title: Rocketship's July 2026 Full Solicits With Emperia, Spirits & Fox Fires
item.description: Rocketship's July 2026 solicitations include new titles in the Emperia series and various graphic novels, expanding their publishing lineup.

item.title: Tesla (TSLA) quietly discloses $2 billion AI hardware company acquisition buried in filing
item.description: Tesla has quietly disclosed a $2 billion acquisition of an unnamed AI hardware company in its Q1 2026 filing, indicating strategic growth in AI.
